author: y4rr
content: Probe-before-fix dump (deeplabv3_remap probe PASSes 3/3): ferrotorch backbone uses 'backbone.layer3.0.conv2.bn.{weight,bias,running_*}' / 'backbone.layer3.0.conv2.weight' for the dilated bottleneck (DilatedConv2d wrapper flattens conv to bare key + bn nested under 'conv2.bn'). Torchvision uses 'backbone.layer3.0.conv2.weight' + 'backbone.layer3.0.bn2.{weight,bias,running_*}'. This is a 6th naming-layout divergence vs the pre-flight's 5-item list, but it is structurally identical to ASPPPooling indexing (also called out as 'handled test-side' in the pre-flight) — the remap function will translate 'backbone.layer<i>.<j>.bn2.<k>' -> 'backbone.layer<i>.<j>.conv2.bn.<k>' for layer3 + layer4 dilated stages. Treating as in-scope for Phase 9's test-side remap (no STOP-AND-REPORT). Layer1/layer2 use standard Bottleneck and follow torchvision's 'bn2' naming directly.
